author: admin#1742094092

text: The Slow Suic*de of the Modern World A Consumption Collapse: The real threat isn't a lack of workers, but a lack of consumers. Global capitalism is an engine driven by human desire; without people to buy goods and services, the entire financial system fails The Technology Trap: AI and automation cannot fix the problem because machines do not have subjective utility. Robots don't "want" coffee, vacations, or bigger homes, meaning they cannot replace the diverse, unpredictable spending that drives economic growth The Impossible Lever: Governments have only two ways to fight decline: biological(incentivizing more kids) or political (immigration). However, pronatalist policies are failing due to modern lifestyle costs, and immigration often creates social volatility that outpaces cultural integration

text: Hyperliquid Treasury Vehicles Now Hold Nearly 9% of HYPE Float Hyperliquid-linked digital asset treasury companies (DATs) now hold close to 9% of HYPE's circulating supply, a higher float-adjusted share than comparable treasury holdings in BTC, ETH, SOL, and BNB. The analyst noted HYPE is the only DAT asset in their dataset trading at a positive mNAV, which could make it easier for these vehicles to raise capital and continue absorbing supply. If an ETF is approved, new passive inflows would enter a tighter float with existing institutional positioning and ongoing treasury demand, which could support price. — link

text: Europe's Early Bitcoin-Treasury Firm Sequans Sells 1,025 BTC France-listed Sequans Communications (SQNS), which raised $384 million in 2025 to launch a Bitcoin treasury strategy and is among the earlier European public tech firms to adopt BTC as a core treasury reserve, has sold 1,025 BTC and now holds 1,114 BTC. Company filings show Sequans previously disclosed 2,139 BTC as of end-2025; as of April 30, its remaining 1,114 BTC was valued at about $84.9 million. The filings also indicate 817 BTC is pledged as collateral for the remaining $35.9 million of the company's convertible notes. — link

text: Coinbase Plans to Cut 14% of Workforce Coinbase co-founder and CEO Brian Armstrong said the company will cut approximately 14% of its workforce, citing market cyclicality and the accelerating impact of AI on productivity. He added that Coinbase will shift toward a leaner, AI-native structure, including fewer management layers and smaller, more efficient teams. Crypto venture firm Dragonfly partner Omar Kanji commented that Coinbase's planned ~14% workforce reduction could save around $225 million in annual compensation. — link

text: Bullish to Acquire Equiniti in $4.2B Deal, Expanding Into Tokenized Securities Bullish has agreed to acquire Equiniti, a global transfer agent, for approximately $4.2 billion including debt, according to the Wall Street Journal. Following the deal, the companies plan to offer corporate issuers end-to-end tokenization services, including 24/7 trading of securities and stablecoin-based payment and settlement solutions. Equiniti currently serves nearly 3,000 public companies, including Berkshire Hathaway and Moody's. The transaction is expected to close in January 2027, subject to regulatory approvals. — link

text: Polygon Wallet Adds Shielded USDC and USDT Payments Polygon Labs has launched shielded stablecoin payments in its wallet, supporting USDC and USDT. Users can now choose a "Privately Send" option, which routes transfers through Hinkal's shielded pool instead of a standard on-chain transfer. The feature uses zero-knowledge proofs to hide the sender, receiver and amount from on-chain observers. Polygon Labs said each private transaction will undergo KYT screening before execution, and the protocol is non-custodial. — link

text: Highlight Clip Kalshi Founder: All Financial Innovation Was Initially Seen as Gambling On April 9, 2026, Tarek Mansour, founder of Kalshi, responded to questions on whether prediction markets constitute gambling in an interview with Axios. He stated that all types of financial innovation were regarded as gambling at their inception, with the stock market during the South Sea Bubble and grain futures in the early 20th century both facing similar controversies, and society eventually recognized their multiple values beyond speculation. Tarek Mansour argued that gambling and speculation should be precisely defined, and prediction markets should not be simply equated with gambling. — link

text: GSR, One of Crypto's Largest Market Makers, Brings On Standard Chartered's SC Ventures as Its First External Strategic Investor Since 2013 SC Ventures, the investment and fintech arm of Standard Chartered, has made a strategic investment in GSR, one of crypto's largest market makers, becoming the firm's first external strategic shareholder since its founding in 2013. The investment amount was not disclosed. The deal builds on a partnership formed last month, when GSR invested in Libeara, a tokenization platform backed by SC Ventures, as part of its broader push into crypto capital markets. — link
